title: Court Observatory — Ruling
description: Structured factual record of a single Israeli Supreme Court ruling within the project's scope (see METHODOLOGY.md §1).

case_id.description: Official Hebrew case identifier as printed in the ruling, e.g. 'בג"ץ 5658/23'. Use the exact string the court uses.

case_id_slug.description: URL-safe filename slug, e.g. '5658-23'. Must match the filename (without .json).

case_name_he.description: Hebrew case style: petitioner v. respondent.

case_name_en.description: English case style: petitioner v. respondent.

ruling_date.description: Date the final judgment was issued, ISO 8601.

filing_date.description: Date the lead petition was filed. Null if not findable.

panel.description: Justices on the panel that decided this ruling.

role.description: Court role at the time of this ruling. Use 'Retired Justice' for participation within the statutory post-retirement window.

slug.description: Stable identifier for joining with data/justices/<slug>.json.

petitioner_name_he.description: Hebrew name of the lead petitioner (NGO, individual, party, etc.). Use 'בעילום שם' for anonymous if applicable.

petitioner_name_en.description: English transliteration or translation of the lead petitioner's name.

respondent.description: Identity of the primary respondent whose decision is challenged.

respondent_decision_he.description: Hebrew description of the specific government decision being challenged.

respondent_decision_en.description: English description of the specific government decision being challenged.

doctrine_invoked.description: Doctrines explicitly named by the ruling. Do not infer doctrines the ruling did not name. See METHODOLOGY §2.

outcome.description: Disposition of the petition. 'upheld' means the petition was granted; 'dismissed' means denied.

vote_majority.description: Number of justices in the majority/winning position. Null if not findable or if the ruling was unanimous (use vote_majority equal to panel size).

vote_minority.description: Number of justices in dissent or alternative position. Zero for unanimous.

majority_authors.description: Slugs of justices who authored opinions in the majority. Empty if no separate opinions identified.

minority_authors.description: Slugs of justices who authored dissenting opinions.

official_url.description: URL of the official ruling text. Strongly prefer supremedecisions.court.gov.il. See METHODOLOGY §5.

secondary_urls.description: Optional: Versa/Cardozo translations, IDI articles, authoritative archives. Do not include news commentary.

summary_he.description: Factual one-to-two-sentence Hebrew summary. See METHODOLOGY §4 — no editorializing.

summary_en.description: Factual one-to-two-sentence English summary. See METHODOLOGY §4.

predicate_ag_opinion.description: If the cabinet/government acted against a prior Attorney General opinion, brief reference to that opinion (date + topic). Null otherwise.

respondent_body.description: Specific statutory or executive body whose action is being reviewed (e.g. 'Senior Appointments Advisory Committee', 'Gronis Committee'). Distinct from broader respondent category.

compliance_state.description: Has the government complied with the ruling? 'pending' = compliance in progress; 'complied' = effective; 'contested' = ruling stands but defiance signals exist; 'n/a' = no compliance question (petition dismissed).

defiance_signals.description: Free-text note on any government refusal to comply, public statements of intent to defy, or legislative work-arounds. Cite sources in the notes field.

tags.description: Optional editorial tags for cross-cutting filtering. Examples: 'appointment-block', 'security-sector', 'religion-state', 'post-oct7'.

notes.description: Free-text notes on disputed facts, source conflicts, or contextual information that doesn't belong in summary fields. See METHODOLOGY §7.
